0.title: Pass
0.description: Not enough to open: fewer than 13 points (HCP plus length).

3.title: 1♣ opening
3.description: Opening hand without a 5-card major: your longer minor, possibly only 3 clubs ("could be short").

4.title: 1♦ opening
4.description: Opening hand without a 5-card major: your longer minor, 3 cards only when exactly 3–3 in the minors is impossible — usually 4+.

5.title: 1♥ opening
5.description: Opening hand with a 5-card or longer ♥ suit. With two 5-card suits open the higher-ranking.

6.title: 1♠ opening
6.description: Opening hand with a 5-card or longer ♠ suit. With two 5-card suits open the higher-ranking.

7.title: 1NT opening
7.description: A balanced hand (no singleton or void, at most one doubleton) with 15–17 HCP.

8.title: 2♣ opening
8.description: Strong and artificial — says nothing about clubs. Almost game-forcing: 22+ HCP, or a hand within one trick of game. Partner must respond (2♦ is the usual "waiting" reply).

9.title: Weak two: 2♦
9.description: A preempt: a good 6-card ♦ suit and 5–11 HCP, below an opening hand. Takes bidding space from the opponents.

10.title: Weak two: 2♥
10.description: A preempt: a good 6-card ♥ suit and 5–11 HCP, below an opening hand. Takes bidding space from the opponents.

11.title: Weak two: 2♠
11.description: A preempt: a good 6-card ♠ suit and 5–11 HCP, below an opening hand. Takes bidding space from the opponents.

12.title: 2NT opening
12.description: A balanced 20–21 HCP. Not forcing; partner may pass with a very weak hand.

13.title: Preempt: 3♣
13.description: A weak hand with a 7-card ♣ suit — obstruction, roughly "within 2–3 tricks of your bid". Usually 5–10 HCP with most strength in the suit.

14.title: Preempt: 3♦
14.description: A weak hand with a 7-card ♦ suit — obstruction, roughly "within 2–3 tricks of your bid". Usually 5–10 HCP with most strength in the suit.

15.title: Preempt: 3♥
15.description: A weak hand with a 7-card ♥ suit — obstruction, roughly "within 2–3 tricks of your bid". Usually 5–10 HCP with most strength in the suit.

16.title: Preempt: 3♠
16.description: A weak hand with a 7-card ♠ suit — obstruction, roughly "within 2–3 tricks of your bid". Usually 5–10 HCP with most strength in the suit.

18.title: Preempt: 4♣
18.description: A weak hand with a long, strong ♣ suit (usually 8 cards) — you expect to take about 8 tricks with ♣ as trumps and little defense.

19.title: Preempt: 4♦
19.description: A weak hand with a long, strong ♦ suit (usually 8 cards) — you expect to take about 8 tricks with ♦ as trumps and little defense.

20.title: Preempt: 4♥
20.description: A weak hand with a long, strong ♥ suit (usually 8 cards) — you expect to take about 8 tricks with ♥ as trumps and little defense.

21.title: Preempt: 4♠
21.description: A weak hand with a long, strong ♠ suit (usually 8 cards) — you expect to take about 8 tricks with ♠ as trumps and little defense.

22.title: 4NT opening
22.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

23.title: 5♣ opening
23.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

24.title: 5♦ opening
24.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

25.title: 5♥ opening
25.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

26.title: 5♠ opening
26.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

27.title: 5NT opening
27.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

28.title: 6♣ opening
28.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

29.title: 6♦ opening
29.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

30.title: 6♥ opening
30.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

31.title: 6♠ opening
31.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

32.title: 6NT opening
32.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

33.title: 7♣ opening

34.title: 7♦ opening

36.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

37.title: 7NT opening
37.description: Not a standard SAYC opening — very rare; shows a highly distributional hand willing to play here.

bestMeaning.title: 1NT opening
bestMeaning.description: A balanced hand (no singleton or void, at most one doubleton) with 15–17 HCP.

meaning.title: 1NT opening
meaning.description: A balanced hand (no singleton or void, at most one doubleton) with 15–17 HCP.

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

meaning.title: Jacoby transfer to ♠
meaning.description: Artificial: shows 5+ ♠ and asks partner to bid 2♠. Any strength — you may pass the transfer with a weak hand, or continue toward game/slam.

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

0.title: Pass
0.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

11.title: Accepts the transfer to ♠
11.description: Artificial: completes partner's Jacoby transfer by bidding ♠ at the cheapest level. Says nothing extra about your hand — partner is captain and may pass, invite, or drive to game/slam next.

12.title: 2NT
12.description: Natural notrump: balanced values with stoppers, proposing to play here or inviting more.

13.description: Natural: length in ♣. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

14.description: Natural: length in ♦. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

15.title: Raise to 3♥
15.description: Agrees ♥ as trumps and invites or sets the level based on combined strength.

16.title: Super-accept of the transfer
16.description: Jumps a level to accept: shows 4-card support for ♠ and a maximum notrump. Extra shape and strength, inviting partner toward slam.

17.title: 3NT
17.description: Offers to play game in notrump: stoppers in the unbid suits and enough combined strength (~25 HCP).

18.description: Natural: length in ♣. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

19.description: Natural: length in ♦. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

20.title: Raise to 4♥
20.description: Agrees ♥ as trumps and invites or sets the level based on combined strength.

21.description: Natural: length in ♠. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

22.title: Blackwood 4NT
22.description: Asks partner how many aces they hold: 5♣ = 0 or 4, 5♦ = 1, 5♥ = 2, 5♠ = 3. Used when your side has agreed a trump suit and is investigating slam.

23.description: Natural: length in ♣. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

24.description: Natural: length in ♦. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

25.title: Raise to 5♥
25.description: Agrees ♥ as trumps and invites or sets the level based on combined strength.

26.description: Natural: length in ♠. Exact ranges in this sequence are beyond the SAYC pamphlet.

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

meaning.title: 1NT opening
meaning.description: A balanced hand (no singleton or void, at most one doubleton) with 15–17 HCP.

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

meaning.title: Jacoby transfer to ♠
meaning.description: Artificial: shows 5+ ♠ and asks partner to bid 2♠. Any strength — you may pass the transfer with a weak hand, or continue toward game/slam.

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

meaning.title: Accepts the transfer to ♠
meaning.description: Artificial: completes partner's Jacoby transfer by bidding ♠ at the cheapest level. Says nothing extra about your hand — partner is captain and may pass, invite, or drive to game/slam next.

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

meaning.title: 3NT
meaning.description: Offers to play game in notrump: stoppers in the unbid suits and enough combined strength (~25 HCP).

meaning.title: Pass
meaning.description: Nothing more to say: no extra strength or shape worth showing at this point.

meaning.title: Rebid of ♠
meaning.description: Extra length in ♠ (usually 6+ cards).
